topic.what: Nature Reviews Disease Primer providing a definitive contemporary overview of autoimmune gastritis — epidemiology, immunopathogenesis, clinical spectrum, diagnosis, and management
topic.how: Multidisciplinary expert panel synthesizing evidence on autoimmune gastritis from immunology, gastroenterology, and hematology perspectives, with structured diagnostic algorithms and surveillance frameworks
topic.why_matters: Serves as the current reference standard for autoimmune gastritis, addressing the full clinical spectrum from iron deficiency (often the earliest sign) through pernicious anemia to neuroendocrine tumor risk, with actionable diagnostic and surveillance algorithms

characteristics.what_it_measures: Range of clinical manifestations covered from subclinical to neoplastic complications
characteristics.value: Covers iron deficiency, B12 deficiency, pernicious anemia, type I gastric NETs, and gastric adenocarcinoma risk
characteristics.vs_baseline: Broader scope than prior reviews which often focused on pernicious anemia alone
characteristics.context: Disease Primer format provides structured, peer-reviewed consensus across subspecialties

good_for: Definitive reference for autoimmune gastritis pathophysiology and management
Implementing structured diagnostic algorithms in clinical practice
Cancer surveillance protocol design for autoimmune gastritis patients
Understanding iron deficiency as an early manifestation before B12 deficiency
requires: Access to serological testing (anti-parietal-cell antibodies, anti-intrinsic-factor antibodies, gastrin, pepsinogen I/II)
Endoscopic biopsy capability with updated Sydney protocol sampling

novelty: First Disease Primer dedicated to autoimmune gastritis, establishing it as a distinct disease entity rather than a subset of chronic gastritis
Introduces structured diagnostic algorithms integrating serological screening with histopathological confirmation
Highlights iron deficiency as the most common and earliest clinical presentation, preceding classic pernicious anemia
open_problems: Lack of randomized trials guiding surveillance endoscopy intervals
Uncertain prevalence due to underdiagnosis and variable diagnostic criteria across studies
No disease-modifying therapy — current management is limited to replacement and surveillance
Interaction between autoimmune gastritis and concurrent H. pylori infection remains poorly characterized
